github.copilot.chat.codeGeneration.instr.text: This is AccrediTex — a React 19 + Vite + TypeScript + Firebase + TailwindCSS v4 + Capacitor SaaS accreditation platform. Stack: React 19, react-router-dom v7, Zustand v5, Firebase v12 (Firestore + Auth + Storage), TailwindCSS v4, Framer Motion v11, TipTap v3, Recharts v3, Capacitor v8 (iOS + Android). Testing: Jest + Testing Library + Playwright. Deployment: Firebase Hosting + Render.

Always use the Agentica v2 MCP tools: call 'reasoningbank_retrieve' before planning any feature, 'router_route' before starting work, and 'reasoningbank_record' after completing tasks. 20 agents available: frontend-specialist (React/UI), backend-specialist (Firebase/API), mobile-developer (Capacitor), database-architect (Firestore schema), debugger, security-auditor, devops-engineer, test-engineer, qa-automation-engineer, performance-optimizer, orchestrator, project-planner, explorer-agent, code-archaeologist, documentation-writer, penetration-tester, game-developer, seo-specialist, product-manager, product-owner.

AccrediTex coding rules: (1) State: always use Zustand stores in src/stores/. (2) Firebase: all queries in src/services/ or src/firebase/ — never in components. (3) Types: strict TypeScript — no 'any'. All types in src/types/. (4) Routing: react-router-dom v7 in src/router/AppRouter.tsx. (5) i18n: all user-facing strings must use the i18n system in src/i18n/. (6) Styling: TailwindCSS v4 utility classes + brand tokens (brand-primary, brand-background, dark:dark-brand-*). (7) NEVER use purple/violet colors — the brand uses custom brand-primary. (8) Mobile: when building Capacitor features, use mobile-developer agent NOT frontend-specialist.

Test rules: Unit tests with Jest in src/test/. E2E with Playwright in e2e/. Follow AAA pattern. Mock Firebase with jest.mock('@/firebase/firebaseConfig'). Coverage target: 80%+. Run: npm run test OR npm run test:e2e.
